def: s.m. Name of a low class of Hindūs.
s.m. Seed of the tamarind.
s.f. Scale or scab (in the head, &c.), a disease that causes the hair to fall off, scaldhead; mange
s.m. Ease, comfort, relief, repose, rest, quiet, calm, peace, tranquillity (syn. ārām
subs: v.n. Ease or comfort, &c. to be obtained, to be at ease or in comfort, &c.
adv. At ease, comfortably; in peace, &c. ( = ārām-se)
v.n. To take ease, enjoy oneself, make oneself comfortable, &c.
vulg. ćain-maʼī, adj. At rest, reposed, tranquil, calm
s.m. Nights of ease or peace, &c.; nights of pleasure or enjoyment, &c.

ety: Prk. चइत्तो or चेत्तो; S. चैत्रः, fr. चित्रा
def: s.m. The twelfth month of the Hindūs (the full moon of which is near ćitrā), March-April
subs: v.n. To join in the songs and sports of ćait or spring
s.f. The spring crop (which is cut in March).

def: s.m. Intelligence, mental perception, reason, understanding, intellect, wisdom, rationality; consciousness, sense, sensation, feeling; animation; caution; the possession of the proper use of the faculties; — soul, spirit; the deity considered as the essence of all being and source of all sensation; — a sentient being; — adj. = ćetan, q.v.
subs: (˚ya+āt˚), s.m. The rational spirit, the living soul

def: s.f. The harvest of the month ćait; the spring crop ( = ćait-kī faṣl).
s.m. See ćait.
s.f. The day of full moon in the month ćait.
s.m. The season or time of ćait, q.v.
s.m. Fissure, cleft, rent, slit, a narrow opening (intentionally left in clothes); — adj. Rent, slit, torn, lacerated
subs: v.t. To rend, tear, slit, split
adj. Having the collar rent; afilicted, sad.

def: s.m. A wheel; a potter's wheel; pulley (over which a well-rope passes); a ring or circle of earth or wood (such as are used in forming a well); a mill; a millstone; a vessel in which sugar is manufactured (after being transferred from the ćāsnī)
subs: The Hindū marriage ceremony of worshipping the potter's wheel.

def: s.m. Landed property, estate, tenure, holding, farm; a piece of assigned land, a patch of rent-free land; a subdivision of land (as of a pargana, &c.); detached fields of a village; cultivation
subs: s.m. Collecting the rent of a ćak
adj. Having an estate, &c. marked out; having the fields, &c. of a village marked out (in a village map)
s.f. Defining or marking the boundaries of an estate or ćak
s.m. A plot or parcel of a landed estate
s.m. A document descriptive of the boundaries of an estate or of fields; an account of village lands, assignments, common pasture grounds, grants, &c.; a grant for holding lands, &c. in a village.

def: s.m. A shepherd; goat-herd; (dialec.) the eye ( = ćakh).
adj. Active, alert; dexterous; erect (a horse), ready (to cover); sound, healthy, hale, hearty; in spirits
subs: adj. Hale and hearty, sound (in health), vigorous, athletic, muscular; well-set, tight; smart, active, alert.

def: s.m. = ćakkā; ćakra, q.v.
s.m. Any circular array of troops.
s.f. = ćakācāk, q.v.
s.f. The clashing of swords, daggers, battle-axes, or maces; the sound of the repeated strokes of a sword, &c.
s.f. The clashing of swords, daggers, battle-axes, or maces; the sound of the repeated strokes of a sword, &c.
s.f. = next, q.v.

s.f. The state of being in a whirl, or dazed, or dazzled; the effect on the eyes of a sudden flash of light; — ćakāćauṅdh lagnā, ćakāćauṅdh-meṅ ānā (-se), To be dazzled or dazed (by); to be confounded or bewildered

s.f. The state of being in a whirl, or dazed, or dazzled; the effect on the eyes of a sudden flash of light; — ćakāćauṅdh lagnā, ćakāćauṅdh-meṅ ānā (-se), To be dazzled or dazed (by); to be confounded or bewildered

s.f. The state of being in a whirl, or dazed, or dazzled; the effect on the eyes of a sudden flash of light; — ćakāćauṅdh lagnā, ćakāćauṅdh-meṅ ānā (-se), To be dazzled or dazed (by); to be confounded or bewildered
s.f. A whirligig; — a species of nectarine.
s.f. The female of the ruddy goose
subs: Male and female (or a pair of) ruddy geese.

def: adj. Broad, wide ( = ćaklā, q.v.).

ety: imperf. part. of ćakīdan, fr. ćak = ćakra = Pārsī śriśk śriśk = Zend śraśka
def: part. adj. Dropping, distilling (used in comp.).
v.t. (dialec.) = ćakhānā, q.v.
s.m. Decision (for ćukāʼo = ćukāʼī).
s.m. Juggling, hocus-pocus, deception, trickery.
s.m.f. A servant, under-servant, an attendant.
adj. Broad, wide ( = ćaklā, q.v.).
s.f. Lands exempted from revenue dues and appropriated to the maintenance of public servants.
s.m. Bustle, tumult, noise, uproar; revelry; — row, quarrel, dispute
subs: v.n. To make a noise or uproar, to cause a tumult; to revel, to riot.

def: s.m. = ćakarbā, q.v.
s.f. Service, employ, office; attendance; duty; a grant for personal service in a village, service-land
s.f. corr. of ćākrī, q.v.
s.m. One habituated to service; one in want of service; one fit for service, a good servant.
s.f. = ćākrī, q.v.
adj. Whirling round, in a whirl, bewildered, dazed, dazzled, astonished
subs: v.n. To be or become dazed or dazzled, to be in a whirl, to be or become astonished or amazed, &c.

def: v.n. To be in a whirl, to be dazzled, &c. ( = ćakāṭe jānā, q.v.s.v. ćakāṭā).
s.m. = ćag̠atta, q.v.
s.m. A round slice (as of a vegetable, &c.), a slice; round discoloured spot (on the body), a mark, scar, scratch or scrape (of the skin); a sod or turf
subs: (meṅ), To bite so as to leave a mark; to mark, to scar
(or ćaktā) denā (-ko), To scar, to mark

def: s.m.(f.?) = H چکونڐ चकवंड ćakwaṇḍ, चकौंड ćakauṅḍ, s.m. = H چکونڙ चकौंड़ ćakauṅṛ, चकोंड़ ćakoṅṛ,चकवंड़ ćakwaṅṛ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).

s.m.(f.?) = H چکوندا चकौंदा ćakauṅdā, चकूंदा ćakūṅdā, s.m. = H چکونڐ चकवंड ćakwaṇḍ, चकौंड ćakauṅḍ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).
s.m. (prov.) = H چکوته चुकौता ćukauta, s.m. = H چکوتي चुकौती ćukautī, s.f. = ćukautā, q.v.
s.m. The ruddy goose (commonly called the Brāhmanī duck; syn. ćakwā; surḵẖāb); — a lark.
s.m. Ringbone (in a horse's foot; — syn. Jakrā wal).
s.f. A kind of ringworm.
adj. (f. -ī), Glittering, shining brilliantly, resplendent, dazzling.
s.f. A kind of musical instrument made of wood (used by Mǒg̠als).
s.f. A kind of dagger (worn on the waist); a small knife (cf. ćāk, ćāqū, ćakkū).
s.f. = ćaqćaqī, q.v.
s.f. = ćakācauṅdh, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Dazzied (by light); weak-eyed, dim-sighted, purblind.

ety: see ćakāćauṅdh, of which it is a var. form
def: s.f. The musk rat, Sorex cœrulescens, or S. cœrulens (syn. ćhućhūṅdarī).

ety: see ćakāćauṅdh, of which it is a var. form
def: s.f. The musk rat, Sorex cœrulescens, or S. cœrulens (syn. ćhućhūṅdarī).
s.m. A kind of swing, a merry-go-round.
adj. & s.m. Having large eyes, goggle-eyed; — a goggle-eyed person.
adj. & s.m. Having large eyes, goggle-eyed; — a goggle-eyed person.
s.m. The blue jay (syn. nīl-kanṭh).

ety: Prk. चक्खू or चक्खुं; S. चक्षुस्
def: s.m. The eye.
adv. (dialec.) = jaisā, q.v. (prob. connected with ćakh = S. ćakshus).
s.f. = ćaḵẖā-ćaḵẖī, q.v.s.v. ćaḵẖ.
s.f. Enmity, opposision, variance, strife, altercation, quarrel, contest; tumult, uproar, noise

ety: ćahk˚ = ćahuk = ćaʼuk or ćauk, q.v.+aiṭ = ait = Prk. अइत्तो+आयंतो = आंतो = S. आपयनआ (the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: adj. Square-built, stout, brawny, sturdy, robust.
s.f. Enmity, opposision, variance, strife, altercation, quarrel, contest; tumult, uproar, noise
v.t. To cause to taste, to make (one) taste, give (one) a taste of; to cause to experience or suffer, to inflict (pain, punishment, &c.).

ety: ćakh(nā)+autī = Prk. आवतिआ = S. आपि+ति+इका (i.e. आप्तिका)
def: s.f. Dainty, delicacy, bonne-bouche, tit-bit.
intj. Begone! be off! away! avaunt! (used by women)
s.m. A jester.
s.f. = ćaqmāq, q.v.
v.t. To taste, &c. ( = ćakhnā, q.v.); to try, test, prove ( = parakhnā).
v.t. To taste, to eat; to relish, enjoy; to experience, suffer
subs: (-ko), To eat up the whole (of a thing)
v.t. To taste or partake of with relish or gusto; to enjoy thoroughly or to the full, to take (one's) fill of, to drain to the dregs.

def: s.m. & adv. = ćakh, q.v.
s.f. = ćakkī, q.v.
part. Dropped, distilled, oozed out.
s.f. Dropping, distillation; a drop.

ety: abst. s. fr. ćak-īdan; see ćakān
def: s.f. The dropping or dripping (of water, &c.).
part. adj. Alarmed, terrified, frightened; astonished, confounded, bewildered.
wety: Learned borrowing from Classical Sanskrit चकित (cakita, “trembling; (by extension) frightened, startled, shocked”). By surface analysis, چَکْ (cak, stem of چَکْنا (caknā, “to be surprised, bewildered or amazed”)) + ـِت (-it, “-ed”).

def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Circular, round; — thick, clotted ( = ćakkān); — a circle; wheel (of a cart, &c.); anything round and flat; a sod of turf; a round or square heap (of material, masonry, &c.); the weight (generally of clay) used to press down the small arm of the ḍheṅklī; — thick curd, coagulated milk.
s.m. = ćakkā, q.v.
adj. Thick, clotted, coagulated, conglomerated; blotted, smeared; — s.m. A blot; a scar, scratch, a round mark.
s.m. A perch for birds (particularly falcons); a roost.
s.m. The loss of a ćak or whole plot of ground by diluvion or inundation.
wety: Learned borrowing from Classical Sanskrit चकित (cakita, “trembling; (by extension) frightened, startled, shocked”). By surface analysis, چَکْ (cak, stem of چَکْنا (caknā, “to be surprised, bewildered or amazed”)) + ـِت (-it, “-ed”).

def: s.m. A Hindū festival at which it is unlawful to eat any grain ground in a mill.
s.f. A mill-stone; a pair of mill-stones, a mill, hand-mill, quern; a grinder, a grinding-stone; — a whirligig; — the timber of a gun-lock; the knee-pan; — a thunderbolt
subs: To grind in a mill, to work a mill or quern
ćakkī jhonā, To turn or work a hand-mill, to grind
s.m. One whose business it is to notch mill-stones
s.m. The upper, or the nether, mill-stone
s.m. Turning or grinding in a hand-mill; (fig.) a grinding task, a tedious or wearisome occupation
s.m. A kind of song sung by women at weddings while grinding a perfumed powder (abṭan)
s.f. Presentation of perfumed powder to the bride and bridegroom and the women by whom it has been prepared.

def: s.m. A pen-knife; a clasp-knife.

adj. Round, circular; — wide, broad; — a round and broad stone or slab, or piece of wood; a block of stone; a log of wood; a mill (for grinding pulse); — a division of a country containing several parganas; quarter of a town (particularly the quarter inhabited by prostitutes); a brothel; — a kind of cloth made of silk and cotton ( = ćakrī)
subs: s.f. The distribution of a province or of a zamīn-dārī into ćaklas
s.m. The expenses of a ćaklā
s.m. The governor or superintendent of a province or ćaklā; the proprietor or renter of a ćaklā
s.f. Government of a province or ćaklā; the status or office of a governor of a ćaklā
s.m. The accountant of the revenues and charges of a ćaklā.

def: s.f. Breadth, width.

ety: ćaklā, q.v.+ān = Prk. अअणं = अत्तणं = S. अ+त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. & f. Breadth, width (syn. ćaklāʼī).

ety: ćaklā, q.v.+ānā = ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā
def: v.t. To extend in breadth, to widen.
s.f. A pulley (over which a well-rope passes).
s.m. A game at cards; — cheating, trickery, trick; loss, injury, damage
subs: A trick to succeed to be successful in cheating or trickery
(-ko), To practise a trick (on), to trick, to cheat; to inflict loss (on), to occasion loss or damage (to).

def: s.m. = ćakma, q.v.
s.m. A boot, a stocking (syn. moza).
s.f. A flint, or steel, for striking fire
subs: To strike fire with a flint and steel.
wety: Borrowed from Chagatai چقمق (čqmq). Compare Turkish çakmak.

def: s.f. = ćaqmāq, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Chagatai چقمق (čqmq). Compare Turkish çakmak.

def: s.f. = H چکمك ćakmak, s.f. corr. of ćaqmāq, and ćaqmaq, qq.v.
s.f. = H چکماك ćakmāk, s.f. corr. of ćaqmāq, and ćaqmaq, qq.v.
s.f. A firelock.
v.t. = ćākhnā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.) = ćakhnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀡 (cikkaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चिक्कण (cikkaṇa).

ety: S. चखण्ड, intens. fr. rt. खण्ड्+चूर्णं
def: adj. & s.m. Broken to pieces, dashed to pieces, shattered, shivered; racked with pain (the body); small pieces, scraps, atoms, filings
subs: v.t. To break or dash to pieces, to shatter, shiver

def: s.m. (dialec.) = ćakr, q.v.
s.m. The Bartavelle or Greek partridge, Perdix rufa, or Tetrao rufus (fabled to subsist upon moon-beams, and to eat fire at the full moon).
s.f. The female of the ćakor, q.v.
s.m. A fruit of the lime kind, the shaddock, pumelo, or pomplemoes, Citrus decumana (said to have been introduced into India from Java).
s.m. A fruit of the lime kind, the shaddock, pumelo, or pomplemoes, Citrus decumana (said to have been introduced into India from Java).
s.m. A kind of flat round cake made of flour and pulse (dāl or baṛā).

s.m. Wheel (of a cart, &c.), a potter's wheel; a catharine-wheel; a discus or sharp circular missile weapon; a quoit; an oil-mill; a circle, a ring; circumference; a circular road or course; a circular position; a form of military array (in a circle = ćakābū); circular flight (of a bird, &c.); revolving in a circle, revolution, whirl; round, circuit; circumambulation; a whirlwind; a whirlpool, an eddy, anything revolving in a circle; a whirligig; the lounge (in a manege); giddiness, vertigo; — an army, a multitude; a side, a quarter (e.g. ćahoṅ cakkar-meṅ, 'On all four sides'); (in Gram.) a table or paradigm; (in Astron.) the zodiac with its twelve signs; (fig.) maze, perplexity; misfortune, scrape
subs: (ćakra+āyudha), adj. & s.m. Fighting with the discus; — an epithet of Vishnu
s.m. 'The line which bisects a circle,' a diameter
(-kā), To whirl (a thing) rapidly round (so as to make it present the appearance of (a disc)
s.f. Circular motion, rotatory motion, rotation
adj. (f. -ī), Touching a circle, tangential; — ćakr-chūtī lakīr, s.f. A tangent
s.m. An epithet of Vishnu (lit. 'bearing a discus'); — a peacock; — a circular course; the lounge (in a manege)
v.n. To be put into rotatory motion, to whirl, turn round, revolve; to move or fly in a circle; to go a long round, take a circuitous road
v.n. To wheel round, to whirl round, to revolve, to move or fly in a circle; to take a roundabout road
s.m. Trick, fraud, deception, evasion, subterfuge, shuffle
(-ke), To be involved in difficulties, be entangled in the meshes (of)
(-ko), To involve one in difficulty or mess; to perplex, bewilder, mislead, lead astray
adv. Revolving like a wheel, in rotation

vulg. ćakar-batti, s.m. Sovereign of the world, universal monarch, emperor, the ruler of a ćakra or country described as extending from sea to sea (twelve princes beginning with Bharat are especially considered as ćakra-vartins); the title of one of the great families of Brāhmans in Bengal
s.f. Interest upon interest, compound interest; — wages for transporting goods in a cart.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चक्र (cakra). Doublet of چرخہ (carxa), چاک (cāk), چکر (cakr), and چَکَّہ (cakka).

def: adj. See s.v. ćakr.

ety: S. चक्र+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To turn round, whirl, &c. ( = ćakkar khānā, q.v.s.v. ćakkar); — to be in a whirl, to feel giddy or dizzy; to be perplexed, to be bewildered or confounded; to be agitated, be disturbed or troubled; to be alarmed or frightened.
s.f. A woman-servant, maid-servant.
s.m. A ring-bone (in a horse's foot, = ćakāwar, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Round, circular.
adj. = ćakeṭhā, q.v.

s.f. A pulley; a mill ( = ćakkī); — a round plate on which bread is rolled; a whirligig; a bandalore or small wheel with a cord fixed to its centre which winds and unwinds itself alternately by the motion of the hands; a circle or an assembly of singers; — a kind of cloth made of silk and cotton.
adj. = ćakit, q.v.
s.m. = ćakwā, q.v.
s.m. = ćaskā, q.v.
s.m. The eye
subs: s.m. Disease of the eye, ophthalmia.

def: adj. Consisting in sight, depending on or produced from sight; peculiar to the eye, belonging to the eye, visual, optical; perceptible by the eye, visible; — relating to Manu ćākshusha; — s.m. Name of the sixth Manu.

adj. Consisting in sight, depending on or produced from sight; peculiar to the eye, belonging to the eye, visual, optical; perceptible by the eye, visible; — relating to Manu ćākshusha; — s.m. Name of the sixth Manu.

adj. Consisting in sight, depending on or produced from sight; peculiar to the eye, belonging to the eye, visual, optical; perceptible by the eye, visible; — relating to Manu ćākshusha; — s.m. Name of the sixth Manu.
s.m. A medicine (made of small black seeds) for diseases of the eye.
s.f. A species of grass.

ety: ćaktā, q.v. = ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To sod, to turf.

ety: for ćakattī = S. चक्र+पत्त्त्रिका; see ćaktā
def: s.f. A small round piece or patch (of leather, &c.); a slice; a round flat lump (as of cheese, soap, tobacco, &c.); a round plate (of metal); a shield; the hide of a rhinoceros (of which shields are made); the tail of the sheep called dumba; the crown of the head; a letter.
s.m. A clasp-knife; a penknife.
s.m. = ćāqū, q.v.
s.m. The ruddy goose, commonly called the Brāhmanī duck, Anas casarca
subs: (ćakwā+anḍa), s.m. The egg of the ćakwī

def: s.m. A whirlpool, an eddy.
s.m. Name of a small class of Rājpūts in the district of Ghāzīpur.

s.m.(f.?) = H چکوندا चकौंदा ćakauṅdā, चकूंदा ćakūṅdā, s.m. = H چکونڐ चकवंड ćakwaṇḍ, चकौंड ćakauṅḍ, s.m. = H چکونڙ चकौंड़ ćakauṅṛ, चकोंड़ ćakoṅṛ,चकवंड़ ćakwaṅṛ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).

s.m.(f.?) = H چکوندا चकौंदा ćakauṅdā, चकूंदा ćakūṅdā, s.m. = H چکونڙ चकौंड़ ćakauṅṛ, चकोंड़ ćakoṅṛ,चकवंड़ ćakwaṅṛ, s.m. The plant Cassia tora (esteemed a remedy for ringworm); — a common weed bearing a long legume (used as a pot-herb).
s.f. The female of the ćakwā, q.v.
s.m. The thatch or roof of a house.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *चल्ला (callā), from Sanskrit *चल्या (*calyā), from चर्या (caryā), from चर् (car, “to move, proceed”) + -या (-yā).

def: s.f. A colour (in horses), roan (cf. ćāṅglā).
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *चल्ला (callā), from Sanskrit *चल्या (*calyā), from चर्या (caryā), from चर् (car, “to move, proceed”) + -या (-yā).

def: intj. Be off! &c.
subs: ćal-ćaṭak, ćal-ćaḵẖe, ćal-dūr, intj. Be off! away! begone! avaunt!

def: s.f. Motion, movement, gait, walk, carriage; pace (of a horse); a move (in chess, &c.); course, procedure, process, mode, method, manner, fashion; rule, practice, custom, usage; conduct, behaviour, habit; breeding, politeness; means, expedient, plan, scheme, device, artifice, manœuvre, trick, stratagem; a metre; a tune, an air; — a kind of fish
subs: (-kī), To follow the course, or practice, or fashion, &c. (of); to adopt the custom, &c. (of); to become current, to prevail
vn. To behave, act, conduct oneself; to practise tricks or deception (on, -se), to behave deceitfully (towards)
(-kī), To show the paces (of); to go away, be off
s.f. Gait, motion, procedure; manners, behaviour, deportment; breeding, politeness; fashion, make, frame
(-kī), To get (at); to smell a rat
(-kī), To fall into the scheming (of), to be tricked or deceived (by).
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *चल्ला (callā), from Sanskrit *चल्या (*calyā), from चर्या (caryā), from चर् (car, “to move, proceed”) + -या (-yā).

def: s.m. Motion; departure; an auspicious moment or day for setting out on a journey (there are different days for different quarters of the compass); the departure of a bride or wife for the home of her husband; the ceremony of the wife's going to and returning from her father's home shortly alter marriage (four such visits are made; see ćār-ćāle); — stratagem, trick; misfortune, calamity.
s.f. A species of fish, a kind of sprat, Clupea cultrata.
s.m. = ćālā, q.v.

ety: masc. in S., but fem. in H., in which it is the v.n. of ćalnā, q.v.
def: adj. Moving, trembling, tremulous, shaking, loose; unsteady, inconstant, fickle, fluctuating; disturbed, confused, troubled
subs: (S. ćala+aćala), adj. Movable and immovable, locomotive and stationary

ćalāćalī (ćala+ćala), adj. & s.f. Moving to and fro; movable, tremulous, shaking, unsteady; variable; — motion to and fro; preparation for departure or for a journey, stir, bustle; setting out on a journey; departure from this life

(S. ćala+vi-ćala), adj. & s.m.f. Unsteady, unsettled, unstable; shaky, rickety, out of joint; displaced, disordered, disarranged; — unsteadiness, inconstancy, unstability, variableness, mutability; shuffling, prevarication; — displacement, derangement; disorder, confusion; blunder, error, mistake
s.m. Motion to and fro, motion
adj. & s.m. Fickle-minded, inconstant, mutable; — a fickle person; — fickleness, mutability, &c. (see next)
s.f. = ćal-ćittatva, s.m. Fickleness of mind, unsteadiness, inconstancy, mutability, frivolity
s.m. The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa
(ćala+indriya), adj. & s.m. Having unsteady organs, sensitive; sensual, not having subdued senses or passions; — a sensual person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Not true to (one's) word; taunting, sneering; — a liar, a shuffler.

def: adj. Active, alert, fleet, nimble, quick, smart; expert, dexterous; clever, ingenious; laborious, hard-working; vigilant; artful, cunning, designing, astute
subs: adj. Expert of hand, dexterous, adroit, clever
s.f. Sleight of hand, expertness, adroitness, dexterity, cleverness
s.m. pl. Astute or designing people; sharpers.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Gone, started, set out, &c.; (see ćalnā)
subs: v.n. To come on or down, to continue (from some past time), to be handed down
v.n. To go away, depart, be off; to go on or along, to proceed, to continue; to last
ćale ćalnā, v.n. To go on, be going on, to keep moving or going, to keep on (a road, walk, &c.), to continue to be or to remain (on a course, or according to a rule).

def: adj. & s.f. See s.v. ćal.
adj. & s.f. See s.v. ćal.
adj. Moving, going, proceeding; movable; unsettled, unsteady, vacillating; inconstant, fickle
adj. & s.m. Laxative; — a laxative.
adj. Active, alert, fleet, nimble, quick, smart; expert, dexterous; clever, ingenious; laborious, hard-working; vigilant; artful, cunning, designing, astute
subs: adj. Expert of hand, dexterous, adroit, clever
s.f. Sleight of hand, expertness, adroitness, dexterity, cleverness
s.m. pl. Astute or designing people; sharpers.

def: s.f. Activity, alertness, smartness; quickness, speed, agility, celerity; expertness, dexterity, skill; cleverness, ingenuity; sharpness; vigilance; astuteness, artfulness, cunning; sharp practice, trickery; stratagem, manœuvre
subs: adv. Astutely, artfully, craftily, by unfair means
(-se), To practise cunning or artfulness, to overreach; to be beforehand (with); to avoid or elude by artifice.

def: s.f. = ćālākī, q.v.

ety: S. चालनं rt. चल्; or i.q. ćālānā = ćalānā, q.v.
def: s.m. An invoice or way-bill, bill of lading; a list (of letters, &c. sent); a certificate of despatch; a remittance; a memorandum of money received and invested; a pass, a passport; clearance (of a ship); sending up or forwarding (a case, or a prisoner, &c. to a magistrate, &c.); transportation
subs: s.m. The person who has charge of an invoice, the bearer of a despatch or remittance; an escort
v.t. To send up (a case, or a prisoner, &c. to a magistrate), to commit (a prisoner) for trial; to forward an invoice (of).

def: s.m. Motion; shaking; sifting; — a sieve; a strainer ( = ćālanī)
subs: s.m. A sifter, one who sifts.

def: s.m. Going, proceeding, departing; course, procedure, process; gait, carriage; manner of life, conduct, behaviour, mien, deportment; habit, custom; ceremony; way, fashion, manner, mode; currency (of money); method, management (of expenditure, &c.), economy; — adj. Tremulous, shaking; unsteady; customary, fashionable, current
subs: v.n. To proceed in a course, &c.; to behave
adv. After a good fashion or manner, with propriety, carefully; methodically, with good management, economically
adj. & s.m. About to go or depart; about to decamp or abscond; about to die, moribund; — one who is about to depart, &c.

def: s.m.(f.?) = ćālān, q.v.

ety: ćalā˚ = Prk. चलाव(इ), or चलावे(इ) = S. चल+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To cause to move or go or pass, to move, put in motion, set agoing; to stir, agitate (with a spoon, &c.); to hasten, urge, impel, propel, drive; to discharge (an arrow or gun), to fire (a gun or cannon), to shoot (at, -par), to throw (a dart, stone, &c.); — to originate, introduce, bring into use; to promulgate; to accustom; to make current, give currency to, put in circulation, to issue, utter (as coin, &c.), pass; — to transmit, send, forward; to advance or lend (money, — e.g. sūd-par rupayā ćalānā); to do, act, transact, carry on, conduct, manage, direct (a work, business, &c.); to prosecute, pursue, carry out; to put in execution, to bring into effect, to enforce; to use, employ, apply; — to thrust (into), drive (in)
wety: From Sanskrit चिल्ल (cilla) (kite (bird)) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. ćalan.
s.f. A kind of short petticoat (worn by women of the humbler classes); cf. ćaṛhnā.
s.f. A riddle; a sieve, a strainer; an instrument for sifting flour; a kind of basket for winnowing grain.
adj. (f. -ī), Passable (as coin); vendible, saleable ( = ćaltā).

ety: ćalā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं or इअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Motion, movement, going; pace; procedure, conduct; habit, custom (see ćāl).
part. Going, moving, &c. ( = ćaltā, q.v.).
s.f. Movement, &c.

adj. Moving, going, passing, shifting; shaky, unstable; mutable, fickle, inconstant; transitory, frail, perishable; passing away, dying; — that may be made to answer or do, that may serve as a make-shift; — durable, lasting.
s.m. An instrument (as a spoon, stick, &c.) for stirring the contents of a pot on the fire; a winch or small stick with which the spinning wheel is turned.
s.m. = ćalāʼo, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = ćalān, ćālān, q.v.
v.t. = ćalānā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Restless, impatient, restive.

ety: ćal-bidhrā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. ववट्टी = S. वृत्तिः
def: s.f. Impatience, restiveness.
s.m. See s.v. ćal.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. ćal.
s.m. See s.v. ćal.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. ćal.
s.f. A detachment or party of men told off for any duty; — the bamboo-work on the floor of a boat; — the saddle of harness.

ety: rt. of ćālnā+Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, artful, astute; roguish, wicked, mischievous; deceitful, treacherous; — an artful person, &c.
s.m. A cross, crucifix; anything crooked or bent.
adj. Forty
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of forty sers; weighty; pure, unadulterated; heavy, stupid
adj. & s.m. Having many (lit. forty) pillars; — a pavilion; a palace, &c.

def: s.m. The fortieth year of any era, or of the century; an aggregate of forty; a period of forty days; a quarantine; — a hero or winner of forty fights; — the great famine (in India) of 1783 (Sambat 1840); — a dimness of sight which is supposed to come on at forty years of age (and often to be removed on approaching the forty-eighth or fiftieth year); — adj. Numbering forty years.
s.f. Elegant gait, graceful motion; stateliness; manner, conduct; — exertion in battle; battle.
s.f. Quarantine; a period of forty days abstinence (kept after the death of a kinsman, or after a childbirth, &c.).

ety: ćālīs, q.v.+Prk. अमओ or अवँओ = S. तम+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -īṅ), Fortieth; — the fortieth day after childbirth, or after the death of a relation (when food is distributed).
part. adj. & s.m. Gone, departed; — shaking, trembling; — current, in vogue, usual, customary; — movable or personal property, &c.
s.m. Conduct; story, &c.
adj. & s.m. Gay, sportive; affected; changeable, inconstant; artful, cunning; — an affected person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Gay, sportive; affected; changeable, inconstant; artful, cunning; — an affected person, &c.

ety: rt. of ćālnā+Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, artful, astute; roguish, wicked, mischievous; deceitful, treacherous; — an artful person, &c.

ety: ćāl˚ = Prk. चाल(इ), or चाले(इ) = S. चालय(ति), caus. of rt. चल्
def: v.t. To cause to pass through a sieve, to sift, to shake; — v.n. To practise artifice or tricks; to be mischievous.

ety: Prk. चलणअं; S. चलनीयं, rt. चल्
def: v.n. To move, stir; to go, proceed; to depart; to walk, travel; to flow, run (as a stream, a pen, &c.); to be loose (as the bowels); to blow (as wind); to sail (as a ship); to be discharged, be fired, to go off (as a gun); to be used, be brandished (as a sword); to set out, to start; to begin, be begun, be introduced; to pass (as coin), to be current, be in vogue, be in operation, be in force; to walk (in, -par, a path or course), to conduct or comport oneself, to behave; to go on well, to flourish, thrive (as a business, &c.); to progress, advance; to go beyond, to exceed (due bounds); to go on, continue (from some past time), be transmitted or handed down; to last, endure; to go down, be taken (as medicine); to pass, work, answer, serve, be of avail, to avail (against); to try tricks or pranks (with or on, -se), to practise trickery or deceit (on); — to move (mentally towards, -par, with the desire of possessing, &c.); — to go, give way, slip, begin to fall; to begin to wear out; — to be made out, be deciphered (as the letters, writing, &c.); — to be thrust (in), be driven (into)
subs: v.n. To leave and abide elsewhere; to depart this life, to die; to come to an end, to be over

v.n. To set out, to start, &c.; — to set in; to come into season, be in season or in the market (as fruit, &c.): ćal-jānā, v.n. (intens. of ćalnā), To pass, work, answer, succeed, avail; to be loose (as the bowels); to go, give way, to fall; to be worn out; to go bad, be spoiled, to turn (as food, milk, &c.); to go mad, lose (one's mind; e.g. dil ćal-gayā)
v.n. To go away, to be off, to decamp
v.n. To move on or away, to march off, to go or take one's way; — to exceed bounds, to turn out vicious.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀮𑀤𑀺 (caladi), from Sanskrit चलति (calati), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷél-e-ti, from *kʷel- (“to move; to turn (around)”). Compare Persian چلیدن (čalīdan, “to go; to walk”), Persian چالاک (čâlâk, “fast, agile, clever”).

def: adj. Current, circulating (as coin); in use, in operation; usual, fashionable.
s.f., contrac. of ćālnī, q.v.
intj. Come! let us be going! away! begone (cf. ćal; ćaliye).

ety: prob. for orig. ćaplak, fr. S. ćap
def: s.f. A thin ćapātī, q.v.; a cake fried in oil or butter.
s.m. A small venomous lizard; a lizard (generally).

ety: imperf. part. of ćalnā; and = Prk. चलंतकः; S. चलत्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Moving, in motion, going, passing, proceeding; movable (as a pulley, &c.); flowing, running (as a stream); running, current (as an account); current, in vogue (as speech, money, &c.); customary, ordinary, usual; in operation, in use; under cultivation (land); passable (as coin, &c.); saleable (as goods); active, busy, flourishing, thriving (as a shop or business); operating quickly, active, efficient, powerful, potent (as a medicine or charm); fast, quick, lively (music, &c.); passing, loose, vague (as speech)
subs: adv. While going, on the way; gradually, by degrees
s.f. Saleable goods, goods in brisk demand; — ćaltī dūkān, s.f. A thriving business
v.t. To keep going, keep up, continue, maintain; to prolong, protract, extend
s.m. Work done hurriedly and carelessly, bad work; temporary work

v.t. To set in motion, set agoing, to start, to begin; to originate, to introduce; to make current, to issue, put forth, send out, despatch; to enable (one) to get on or along; — to set free, to send away, to discharge
adv. With the arms and legs in working condition, while the limbs are serviceable
s.m. A sharp tool or instrument; (fig.) a sharper, cheat, swindler
v.n. To be in motion; to be in operation, &c. (see ćaltā); to be off, to go or run away ( = ćal-denā); to be issued, be sent out; be despatched.

def: s.m. The tree Dillenia indica or D. speciosa (the fruit of which has an agreeable taste; the fleshy leaves of the calyx, which surround the ripe fruit, make a palatable jelly; the wood is used to make gun-stocks, helves, &c.).
adj. = ćalan-hār, q.v.s.v. ćalan.
s.f. Activity, briskness (of business, trade, &c.); access, reach; sphere of action, power, authority, influence, jurisdiction; competency; — land under cultivation ( = ćaltī zamīn).

s.m. A handful or mouthful of water ( = ćullū, q.v.); rinsing the mouth by sipping water from the hand and spitting it out again (a ceremony performed before religious ceremonies, meals, &c.; syn. āćaman).
s.m. The hand hollowed to hold a little water, &c.; a handful of water ( = ćullū); a small pot, a gallipot.
s.f. A kind of sprat or anchovy, Clupea cultrata.
adj. & s.m. Moving about much, wandering about, roving; — one who moves about a great deal, a great walker, &c.
v.t. = ćalānā, q.v.
s.f.m.(?) = ćāl-ćalan, q.v.s.v. ćāl.
s.m. Skin, hide; leather
subs: s.f. Adulterous connexion with another man's wife
s.m. The leather money made current by the bhishtī, Nizām, during his three days rule in the time of the Emperor Humāyūn
To introduce a leather currency; to stretch to the utmost a temporary authority.

def: adj. & s.m. Prepared, adorned, arranged; — gained; — bent, curved, crooked; — an easy air, a graceful gait; a swaying motion in walking.
s.m. An iron mace; a wooden mace or club with a knob on the head, or headed with iron.
adj. Tawny, copper-coloured.
s.m. An iron mace; a wooden mace or club with a knob on the head, or headed with iron.
s.f. Glitter, brilliance, splendour; flash; gleam; — timidity, starting, tendency to shy
s.f. A passionate or quarrelsome woman; — a wanton woman.

ety: ćamak˚ = Prk. चमक्क(इ) or चमक्के(इ), S. चमत्+क्रियते, pass. fr. कृ
def: v.n. To shine, beam, glow, glimmer, sparkle, glisten, glitter, glare; to be bright, be polished; — to do well, prosper, flourish, thrive; to be lively or brisk (as a market, &c.); to prevail, rage (as an epidemic, &c.); to fly into a passion, become angry; — to be startled, to start, shy; — to get to the head (liquor)
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*cammakka), from an onomatopoeic 𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫 (camma) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Related to چونکنا (cauṅknā). Cognate with Punjabi ਚਮਕਣਾ (camkaṇā) / چَمَکنا (camaknā), Gujarati ચમકવું (camakvũ), Marathi चमकणे (ċamakṇe).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Bright, shining, glittering, glistening, splendid; flourishing, prosperous, &c.
v.t. doub. caus. of ćamaknā, q.v.; and also = ćamkānā, q.v.
s.f. = ćampak-baṛnī, q.v.

ety: imperf. part. of ćamīdan; rt. Zend jam — S. गम्
def: s.m. Swaying from side to side in walking; walking jauntily or with a strut, walking haughtily or gracefully.
s.m. A bed (in a garden), flower-bed, a parterre; a flower-garden; a blooming, verdant, or flourishing place
adj. Of or relating to a parterre or flower-bed.
s.m. The tail of the Bos grunniens (used as a whisk to drive away flies, &c.)
subs: s.m. The betel-nut tree, Areca faufel or Catechu.

ety: Prk. चम्मआरो or चम्मआलो; S. चर्म+कारः
def: s.m. A caste of men who work in leather; a member of that caste; a worker in leather, a currier, a shoemaker, harness-maker; a cobbler; a tanner
subs: s.m. A meeting of shoemakers, &c.; — a row, great noise, tumult, din

def: s.m. (f. -ī), The Bos grunniens (syn. yāk, — highly valued for its bushy tail); — the tail of the Bos grunniens (used to whisk off flies, insects, &c.; it is also one of the insignia of royalty), a chowrie, a whisk ( = ćaṅwar)
s.f. 'The disposition of a ćamār'; aversion to labour; sullenness
subs: s.m. The quarter (of a town, &c.) inhabited by ćamārs.

def: (lit. 'That which increases the ćamar or ćambal,' q.v.), s.m. pl. (dialec.), The rain which falls about twelve days after the end of the cold season, winter rains.
s.f. A woman of the ćamār caste; the wife of a ćamār.
s.f. The female of the ćamar or Bos grunniens
s.f. A woman of the ćamār caste; the wife of a ćamār.
s.f. A woman of the ćamār caste; the wife of a ćamār.
s.f. = ćamārnī, and ćamarī, qq.v.
adj. Belonging or appertaining to a ćamār; low, vile; ill-looking; badly made.
s.m. A kind of spoon or ladle (used at sacrifices for drinking the juice of the acid asclepias); — the plant Mollugo pentaphylla (commonly called khet-pāpar).
s.m. Surprise, astonishment, amazement; show, pomp, grandeur, splendour; a spectacle; a miracle; — haste; — a kind of grass.
adj. & s.m. Wonderful, astonishing, surprising, extraordinary, unusual; sagacious, acute; — a wonderful man, a sagacious person, &c.; — s.f. = ćamat-kār.
part. adj. Surprised, astonished, amazed.
s.f. Slippers or shoes fixed to pattens.
s.f. Slippers or shoes fixed to pattens.
s.m. Name of a clan of the Gūjar tribe in Panipat Bargar.
s.m. A class of beggars who squat before a house and cut or scarify their skin for the purpose of extorting alms.
s.m. = چمبا ćambā, q.v.
s.f. Name of a river in Bundelkhand that falls into the Jamnā; — a log of wood with grooves in it, which is fixed on the banks of canals, and used in drawing water for irrigation.
s.m. = ćambar, q.v.
s.m. = ćammal, ćamlā, q.v.
s.m. = ćammal, camlā, q.v.
s.m. See چنبر ćambar.
s.m. (dialec.) = ćamār, q.v.
s.m. Cover for the ćilam of a ḥuqqa (syn. sar-posh); — a circle, hoop (of a tub, &c.), a ring (cf. ćummal)
subs: s.m. Stocks; pillory; a necklace, a handkerchief; a dog's collar.

def: adj. Round, circular.
s.f. = ćamelī, ćaṅbelī, q.v.
s.f. The jasmine, Jasminum grandiflorum ( = ćamelī)
subs: s.m. A kind of embroidery (ćaṅbelī-shaped flowers worked on cloth).

def: s.m. = ćammal, ćamlā, q.v.
s.m. = ćammal, camlā, q.v.
s.m. A narrow-necked vessel for holding water, a gugglet (cf. jambū).
s.m. = چمبو ćambū, q.v.
s.m. A spoon, a ladle
subs: s.m. A sponger; a meddler; a libertine.

def: s.m. A spoon ( = ćamća, q.v.)
s.f. Brightness, sparkle, glitter.
v.n. To tingle, smart; to sleep, become numb (as the limbs); — to shine, sparkle, glitter ( = ćamaknā, q.v.).
adj. Skinny, emaciated, lean, meagre, — s.m. A bat; — a skinny person, a bag of bones.
adj. Skinny, emaciated, lean, meagre, — s.m. A bat; — a skinny person, a bag of bones.
adj. Skinny, emaciated, lean, meagre, — s.m. A bat; — a skinny person, a bag of bones.
s.f. A small spoon (such as is used to take out pickles, or the lime which is eaten with betel).
adj. & s.m. 'Sticking to the skin like a tick,' sticking close; — skinny; — one who sticks close.
s.m. The jasmine, Jasminum grandiflorum (also written ćambelī, ćaṅbelī).
s.m. A slap, box, thump, blow.

s.m. (old H.) = H چمگادڙ चमगादड़ ćamgādaṛ, चिमगादड़ ćimgādaṛ, s.m. = H چمگدڙ चमगिद्दड़ ćamgiddaṛ [S. चर्म+गृध्रः], s.m. = H چمگدڙا चमगिदड़ा ćamgidṛā, s.m. = H چمگدڙي चमगुदड़ी ćamgudṛī, s.f. = H چمگيدڙ चमगीदड़ ćamgīdaṛ, s.m. A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
s.m. (old H.) = H چمگيدڙ चमगीदड़ ćamgīdaṛ, s.m. A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
s.m. (old H.) A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
s.m. (old H.) = H چمگيدڙ चमगीदड़ ćamgīdaṛ, s.m. A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
s.m. (old H.) = H چمگيدڙ चमगीदड़ ćamgīdaṛ, s.m. A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
s.m. (old H.) = H چمگيدڙ चमगीदड़ ćamgīdaṛ, s.m. A flying fox; a bat (also ćamgaddaṛ and ćimgaddaṛ).
adj. Real, true, significant.
s.m. Gold.

ety: ćamkā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and -āhaṭ = āwaṭ = aʼo or āw+Prk. अंट्टं = S. क+त्वं; and -āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Brightness, glittering, glitter, much light, gleam, flash, brilliance, lustre, splendour; — flourish; — starting, start.

ety: ćamkā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and -āhaṭ = āwaṭ = aʼo or āw+Prk. अंट्टं = S. क+त्वं; and -āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Brightness, glittering, glitter, much light, gleam, flash, brilliance, lustre, splendour; — flourish; — starting, start.

v.t. To cause to glitter, cause to beam or glow; to flash; to burnish, brighten, polish; to display, to make a show; to make famous; to cause to flourish or prosper, to extend, to carry to a great height of prosperity; to wave, brandish, flourish; to rouse, stir up, make lively or active; to provoke, to cause to start or shy, to startle
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*cammakka), from an onomatopoeic 𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫 (camma) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Related to چونکنا (cauṅknā). Cognate with Punjabi ਚਮਕਣਾ (camkaṇā) / چَمَکنا (camaknā), Gujarati ચમકવું (camakvũ), Marathi चमकणे (ċamakṇe).

ety: ćamkā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and -āhaṭ = āwaṭ = aʼo or āw+Prk. अंट्टं = S. क+त्वं; and -āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Brightness, glittering, glitter, much light, gleam, flash, brilliance, lustre, splendour; — flourish; — starting, start.
s.m. Glitter, glare, light.
s.m. = ćamkārā, q.v.
adj. Shining, bright, &c. ( = ćamkīlā).
s.f. Glitter, &c. ( = ćamak); — tinsel, a spangle.
adj. (f. -ī), Brilliant, sparkling, glittering, splendid.
s.m. = H چمل चम्मल ćammal, s.m. = H چملي चमली ćamlī, s.f. A beggar's cocoanut-shell or cup; a begging dish.
s.f. = H چمل चम्मल ćammal, s.m. = H چملا चमला ćamlā, s.m. A beggar's cocoanut-shell or cup; a begging dish.
s.m. A spoon ( = ćamća, q.v.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چمچه (čamča, čumča), from Turkic. Compare Sylheti ꠌꠣꠝꠌ (samoso).

def: s.f. (dialec.) = ćambal, q.v.
s.m. = H چملا चमला ćamlā, s.m. = H چملي चमली ćamlī, s.f. A beggar's cocoanut-shell or cup; a begging dish.

ety: S. चर्म+पपट्टिकः or पट्टिका; or dim. aff. oṭā
def: A strap, or piece of leather; a razor-strop; a leather sock (put round the leg of a prisoner, &c.); — ćamoṭā, (fig.) A leather-headed person, a blockhead, dolt.

ety: S. चर्म+पपट्टिकः or पट्टिका; or dim. aff. oṭā
def: A strap, or piece of leather; a razor-strop; a leather sock (put round the leg of a prisoner, &c.); — ćamoṭā, (fig.) A leather-headed person, a blockhead, dolt.
s.m. Mountain ebony, Bauhinia variegata.
s.m. The tree Michelia champaca (bearing a fragrant yellow flower)
subs: s.f. The colour of the ćampā flower ( = ćampāʼī, q.v.); — a name or an epithet of females
ćampā-kālī, s.f. A necklace composed of little ornaments in the form of the bud of the ćampā flower
s.m. (f. -ī), A species of plantain (small, scented, and very sweet).

def: s.m. = چمپا ćampā, q.v.
s.f. = ćampā; — adj. & s.f. = ćampaʼī; ćamāʼī; qq.v.
adj. = چمپئي ćampaʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.f. Of the colour of the ćampā flower, yellow, golden, orange-coloured; — yellow colour, &c.
s.m. The tree Michelia champaca ( = ćampā); — a variety of the jack or bread-fruit tree
s.m. = چمپك ćampak, q.v.

ety: ćamp˚ = S. चम्पय(ति), rt. चम्प् = छम्प्, prob. a Prk. form akin to S. क्षि; cf. ćhipnā
def: v.n. (old H.), To lie concealed, to be hid; to crouch; to creep.
